2d ago

Image 11: Сергей Ескин

Сергей Ескин

The "no feed, no AI" positioning is clear and honestly refreshing for a journaling app. I also like the constraint of one dated page per day; it makes the product feel closer to a real notebook than another notes database.

The sealing choice is brave. Since you deliberately avoided edit exceptions, do you think of follow-up thoughts as today's page referring back to yesterday, rather than corrections on the old page?

Upvote (1)

Report

Share

Image 13: Сергей Ескин

Сергей Ескин

@ishaanrawat

That makes sense. I actually like that constraint: it keeps the product honest as a daily page instead of slowly becoming another notes database. The absence of backlinks/jump buttons may be part of the calmness here, even if it trades off power-user convenience.

results.title: Limitless Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
The hardware pendant (which is their flagship product) is a clever play—you're not expected to be holding your phone constantly, so capturing voice and context becomes more natural. For someone with ADHD or just a busy mind that juggles a thousand threads, this product is genuinely therapeutic. One reviewer nailed it: "I don't have to keep track of a million things at once." That's the win. [...] Also, the subscription pricing (before the Meta acquisition made it free for pendant users) was steep, and the value proposition requires you to truly adopt the habit of constant capture. If you only use it occasionally, you're paying for unused potential. Regional availability is also limited, which matters if you're outside the US or EU.

#### vs Alternatives

AI Hive [...] #### What needs improvement

The elephant in the room: Limitless now reveals that Meta owns it, and they're sunsetting the non-pendant functionality. This creates real uncertainty for users who don't own the pendant hardware. The transcription accuracy, especially across languages, isn't flawless—I tested it with a mix of English and some Vietnamese phrases, and the non-English parts got garbled. Battery drain is a legitimate complaint I can confirm: running Limitless all day definitely impacts your phone's battery life noticeably.
